The largest NATO bases with the most fighters are all US Air Force and US Navy bases, with some having more fighters than entire European air forces.

US military dominance at NATO bases underscores American air power concentration, with some single installations exceeding entire European air force capabilities.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

NASA’s X-59 Experimental Supersonic Aircraft Makes Second Flight

NASA’s quiet supersonic X-59 aircraft made its second flight on Friday, kicking off a series of dozens of test flights in 2026. Although the flight duration was abbreviated due to a technical issue, the team was able to collect information that will inform future tests. “Despite the early landing...

NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic technology demonstrates progress toward overturning sonic boom restrictions, potentially enabling future commercial supersonic routes over populated areas.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

NASA X-59 Second Flight Ends Early After In-Flight Warning

Test campaign set to continue soon despite in-flight issues.

NASA's X-59 experimental aircraft encountered in-flight warnings during its second test flight, but the program remains on track to continue validating quiet supersonic flight technology for future commercial operations.

STELIA will showcase innovative premium cabin solutions at AIX 2026, signaling continued investment in passenger comfort differentiation.

Business Aviation Mar 20, 2026

FlightPath3D’s strategic expansion into BizAv pays off

Though it has largely operated in stealth mode in business aviation for several years, FlightPath3D’s customizable moving maps with cinematic views are now flying on thousands of BizAv tails, and the Lake Forest, California-based firm is getting a little more vocal about its ever-growing footprin...

FlightPath3D's advanced moving map technology is gaining significant market traction in business aviation, demonstrating strong demand for innovative cockpit visualization solutions.

Regulatory Mar 20, 2026

A photojournalist is challenging FAA’s drone ban over DHS vehicles

The FAA is being challenged on a recent flight restriction that banned drone flights over all government buildings and mobile assets. The lawsuit is being managed by The Reporters Committee for Freedom of the Press and is challenging it on both First Amendment and Administrative Procedure Act vio...

FAA's expanded drone restrictions face legal challenge on constitutional grounds, potentially reshaping media access and regulatory authority.

Widebody Safe Haven? Airlines Forced To Ground & Store Aircraft As Iran Conflict Continues

Middle East geopolitical tensions are forcing airlines to ground widebody aircraft, increasing storage costs and reducing fleet utilization during a critical period for international connectivity.

The contract covers the design and construction of an opti...

Laser communication ground stations represent emerging infrastructure enabling satellite-to-ground data transmission capabilities critical for aerospace operations.

Military Mar 20, 2026

Talon IQ Testbed Performs Simulated Combat Maneuvers Controlled by Hivemind and Prism AIs

The Talon IQ testbed conducted combat air patrol and target engagement maneuvers controlled by ShieldAI’s Hivemind AI, before switching back to Northrop Grumman’s Prism AI. Northrop Grumman and Shield AI announced on Mar. 19, 2026, that the Talon IQ autonomy testbed aircraft, based on the Model 4...

AI-controlled autonomous aircraft successfully executed complex combat maneuvers, signaling rapid advancement in unmanned military aviation capabilities and human-AI teaming concepts.

London Heathrow dominates the US market in European aviation, with Charles de Gaulle and Frankfurt far behind.

London Heathrow's transatlantic dominance underscores the strategic importance of hub positioning and bilateral air service agreements for competitive advantage in international markets.

Restoration has been completed on Hangar One, a historic landmark in the San Francisco Bay Area and a key part of the region’s early...

Hangar One's restoration completion preserves critical aviation heritage infrastructure and demonstrates commitment to protecting historic facilities essential to regional aerospace operations.

Industry Mar 21, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through advanced technologies offers airlines, airports, and air traffic management a viable pathway to accommodate growing demand while reducing environmental impact.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

Another GPS launch shifts from ULA to SpaceX as Vulcan investigation continues

The change affects the GPS III SV-10 satellite, which had been slated to launch on ULA’s Vulcan Centaur rocket. The post Another GPS launch shifts from ULA to SpaceX as Vulcan investigation continues appeared first on SpaceNews.

GPS constellation deployment delays due to Vulcan investigation are pushing critical national infrastructure launches toward SpaceX, reshaping launch provider competition.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

NASA’s X-59 cuts second test flight short after return-to-base call

NASA’s X-59 quiet supersonic demonstrator unexpectedly cut short its second test flight on March 20, 2026, after a return-to-base call. The… The post NASA’s X-59 cuts second test flight short after return-to-base call appeared first on AeroTime.

X-59's premature flight termination highlights engineering challenges in supersonic technology development that could impact future commercial supersonic aircraft programs.

Safety Mar 20, 2026

Delta Air Lines Grounds Airbus A319 After Cabin Loses Electrical Power Inflight

The flight made an emergency landing at Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport after the cabin temporarily lost power during its final approach.

Electrical system failures remain a critical safety concern requiring enhanced pre-flight diagnostics and maintenance protocols for aging narrow-body aircraft.

How The Eurofighter Typhoon Stacks Up Against The Sukhoi Su-35 In 2026

Drones Mar 20, 2026

DJI Flip now cheaper than many beginner drones

There’s a reason drones often sit in online carts longer than they should. It’s not just about cost; it’s the friction. Too many controls. Too much setup. Too many things that can go wrong in the first five minutes. DJI Flip was designed to chip away at that hesitation. And now, with a fresh roun...

DJI's price reduction on the Flip model removes cost barriers while addressing user friction points, potentially expanding the consumer drone market beyond hesitant beginners.

NASA's RASC-AL competition develops next-generation aerospace talent while advancing lunar and Martian exploration concepts that may reshape future aviation and space operations.

Army's optionally piloted Black Hawk enables autonomous helicopter testing critical to modernizing rotorcraft operations and reducing pilot workload in hazardous missions.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

How Open NASA Data on Comet 3I/ATLAS Will Power Tomorrow’s Discoveries

The interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS will soon leave our solar system, never to return, but the observations of the comet will live on in NASA’s public data archives. More than a dozen NASA science missions turned their instruments to observe the comet, which is only the third identified object to be ...

NASA's open-source comet observation data demonstrates the aerospace industry's collaborative approach to advancing scientific discovery and technology development.
